facts.text: Union City was originally inhabited by the Munsee-speaking Lenape before Dutch explorer Henry Hudson visited in 1609.
The city earned the nickname “Embroidery Capital of the United States” thanks to its booming textile factories in the early 20th century.
In the late 1940s, Cuban immigrants flocked to Union City for work in embroidery mills, earning the area the moniker “Havana on the Hudson.”
The Union City Artists Collective, founded in 2007, launched the city’s first Art Curator and opened an art gallery inside City Hall.
Saint Michael the Archangel Church on West Street, once Hudson County’s largest Catholic church, is the only Union City site on the National Register of Historic Places.
The Park Performing Arts Center, built in 1931 by a German congregation, now hosts Hudson Theatre Works and seats 1,400 patrons.
